issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not announce dynamic result count
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the product‑count badge (`_updateCountBadge`) but never writes the new count to an ARIA live region, so screen‑reader users are not informed when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, add a hidden element with `aria-live="polite"` (or reuse an existing status element) and set its text to “X products shown”.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic savings message not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateSavingsMessage()` inserts a short “Save 20 % with annual billing” notice into the page, but the element has no `aria-live` attribute, so the change is invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` (or `assertive` if urgent) to the savings message element, or fire `liveRegion.announce()` after setting its text.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in `aria‑invalid` (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit()` the email address is validated with `validateField()`, but when the validation fails the code only shows a status message – it never calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` or sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input type="email">`.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call `srUtils.markInvalid(emailInput, errorMessageElement)` (or `emailInput.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true')`) and ensure the error element is referenced with `aria-describedby`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu opening not announced to screen readers (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger and removes `hidden` from the panel, but it does not provide any live‑region announcement that the submenu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or add an element with `aria-live="polite"` that receives a brief message.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu closing not announced to screen readers (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` mirrors the open logic but also lacks a live‑region announcement that the submenu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')` (or update a status region) after the close operation.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state change not announced (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateEmptyState()` shows or hides the “no results” element, but the element has no `aria-live` attribute, so users are not told when the list becomes empty.
issuesFound.fix: Give the empty‑state container `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` (or call `liveRegion.announce('No products match the filter')`).

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates lack live‑region notification (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.render()` populates the `<ul role="listbox">` with new `<li role="option">` items, but there is no accompanying live‑region message indicating how many suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} suggestion${count===1?'':'s'} found`)` or add an `aria-live` element that receives the count.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger may miss `aria-controls` (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the code only adds `aria-controls` to the trigger **if** the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer always receives a unique `id` (e.g., generate one with `shortId()`) before linking, or always set `aria-controls` after generating the id.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle does not provide live‑region feedback for the change (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `themeSwitcher.set()` announces the change via `liveRegion.announce()`, but the toggle buttons themselves only receive `aria-pressed` updates; the visual label on the button is not announced when the theme changes via keyboard shortcuts that do not activate the button (e.g., programmatic calls).
issuesFound.fix: After any theme change (including programmatic), also update a hidden `aria-live="polite"` element with “Theme switched to Dark/Light/System”.

issuesFound.title: Active‑navigation link changes are not announced (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `activeNavLinks.init()` adds `aria-current="page"` to the newly‑active link, but there is no live‑region notification that the navigation context has moved, which can leave screen‑reader users unaware of the change when navigation is updated via JavaScript (e.g., after a hash change).
issuesFound.fix: After updating the active link, call `liveRegion.announce('Navigation moved to ${link.textContent}')` or attach an `aria-live` element that receives the link’s label.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by adding or removing a CSS class (`openClass`) but the code never sets `aria-hidden="true"` when the drawer is closed nor removes it when opened. This leaves the drawer in the accessibility tree while visually hidden, violating JS‑F.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150: add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in `close()` before adding the hidden class.
    Line 2150: add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` in `open()` after removing the hidden class.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the trigger button (`_trigger`) receives `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the trigger ends up without `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between the control and the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2105: ensure the drawer always has an `id` (e.g., `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer';`) before setting `aria-controls`.
    Then set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);` unconditionally.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_updateCountBadge(count)` updates the text of the product count badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement. Users of assistive technology will not be informed when the number of visible products changes after filtering, violating JS‑G.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3110: add a hidden live region element (e.g., ``). Line 3110: after setting the badge text, also set `document.getElementById('filter-count-live').textContent = `${count} products shown`;`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) toggles the mobile drawer but the script only adds `aria-controls` when the drawer element already has an `id`. The drawer (`#nav-menu`/`#nav-drawer`) has no `id`, so the trigger ends up without an `aria-controls` reference, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form status element lacks `aria-live`
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div>` used to display success or error messages after newsletter submission is given a class and `hidden` attribute, but it does not have `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` so screen‑reader users are not notified of the result.

issuesFound.title: Contact form status element lacks `aria-live`
issuesFound.explanation: After a contact‑form submission the script updates a `<div>` with a success or error message, but the element has only `role="status"` without an `aria-live` attribute, so the announcement may not be spoken.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the element has `aria-live="polite"` (or `"assertive"` for errors):
```js
_statusEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');
```
or create it with those attributes initially.

---

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input status element lacks `aria-live`
issuesFound.explanation: The `<div>` that shows validation feedback for a discount code is given a class but no live‑region role, so users of assistive technology are not informed whether the code was accepted.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload component status element lacks `aria-live`
issuesFound.explanation: The component’s status `<div>` (used for busy/ready feedback) is created without `aria-live`, so screen‑reader users receive no audible cue when a file begins uploading or finishes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls` (duplicate check for robustness)
issuesFound.explanation: The same trigger (`.nav-toggle`) also lacks an explicit `aria-controls` attribute when the drawer element does not have an `id`. This omission appears both in the initialisation and in the toggle‑open/close logic, potentially causing the attribute to be omitted entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Consolidate the fix by ensuring the drawer always receives an `id` and the trigger always receives a matching `aria-controls` attribute, as shown in Issue 1. This guarantees the relationship is present regardless of load order.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in **mobileNav** adds the “open” CSS class and shows the drawer, but it never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state to true, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑pressed when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) toggles the navigation drawer, but the script never sets aria‑pressed to reflect its pressed state, violating the toggle‑button pattern.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The **faqAccordion** open() function expands the answer panel and announces the change, but it never sets aria‑expanded on the question button, leaving the expanded state invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The **fileUploadField** bind() creates a drag‑and‑drop zone with role="button" and tabindex="0", but it never provides an aria‑label or aria‑description, leaving the purpose of the zone unclear to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles light/dark/system themes (identified by [data-theme-toggle]) receives aria‑pressed updates but no aria‑label, so its purpose is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The element with [data-billing-toggle] acts as a switch between monthly and annual billing, but the script never assigns an aria‑label, making its function invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that switch between grid and list views (identified by [data-view-mode]) receive aria‑pressed updates but no aria‑label, so screen‑reader users cannot know which view will be activated.

fpTitles: File‑upload zone lacks an accessible name
Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons lack accessible names

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not update `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function shows the mobile navigation drawer but never sets the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` to `true`. The button is given `aria‑expanded="false"` in `mobileNav.init()`, so the ARIA state becomes out‑of‑sync after the first open, breaking screen‑reader awareness of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, `_openInstance()` adds the `open` class and removes `hidden`, but it never sets the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` to `true`. Only the close path resets it to `false`, leaving the ARIA state incorrect after an open action.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open does not update `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function expands a FAQ panel but never updates the associated button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `true`. Only the close path clears it, causing screen readers to think the panel remains collapsed after it is opened.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance lacks live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown changes visible content (shows the menu) but does not announce the change to assistive technology. No `liveRegion.announce()` or similar call follows the state change, leaving users unaware that a new menu has appeared.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks a field as invalid by adding an error message, but the helper `srUtils.markInvalid()` only adds `aria‑describedby` and never sets `aria‑invalid="true"`. Thus the newsletter email input never receives `aria‑invalid`, violating WCAG 2.1 A/AA.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria‑invalid` on contact form “Full name” field
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” input is validated on blur/input, but `srUtils.markInvalid()` does not set `aria‑invalid`. Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Apply the same change as Issue 5 (add `aria-invalid="true"` in `markInvalid`). No further code change needed per field.

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria‑invalid` on contact form “Email address” field
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 6 for the email input – missing `aria‑invalid` when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix:
Covered by the fix in Issue 5/6.

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria‑invalid` on contact form “Subject” field
issuesFound.explanation: Validation errors are not reflected in `aria‑invalid`.
issuesFound.fix:
Covered by the fix in Issue 5/6.

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria‑invalid` on contact form “Message” field
issuesFound.explanation: Same missing `aria‑invalid` for the message textarea.
issuesFound.fix:
Covered by the fix in Issue 5/6.

---

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set `aria‑invalid` on contact form “Consent” checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The required consent checkbox is validated, but `aria‑invalid` is never set on failure.
issuesFound.fix:
Covered by the fix in Issue 5/6.

---

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close does not announce menu closure
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown hides the menu but provides no live‑region feedback, leaving users unaware that the menu has been dismissed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels do not set `aria‑expanded="true"` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` toggles the panel’s visibility (removes `hidden` and adds a CSS class) but never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `true`. Only the closing path sets `aria‑expanded="false"`.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance()` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` so the button reflects the open state.

---

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button uses `aria‑checked` instead of `aria‑pressed` for a toggle control
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` and `aria‑checked`, but the component is a button that toggles between two states (play / pause). WCAG requires `aria‑pressed` on toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', ...)` with `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', ...)` and keep `role="button"` or `role="switch"` as appropriate.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and inserting an error message, but it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input element (the helper `srUtils.markInvalid` only adds `aria-describedby`). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult()` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when `!result.valid` and remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Search‑suggestion list lacks an ARIA live region for updates
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.mount()` creates the `<ul role="listbox">` but does not give it `aria-live="polite"` or `aria-atomic`. When suggestions appear or change, screen‑reader users receive no audible notification.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-live="polite"` (and optionally `aria-atomic="true"`) to the listbox element when it is created.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger button is missing `aria-controls` when the drawer has no `id` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: During `mobileNav.init()` the script sets `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id` (the default markup does not include one), the trigger has no relationship to the controlled region, breaking the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Generate a unique `id` for the drawer (e.g., `_drawer.id = shortId('nav-drawer')`) and always set `aria-controls` on the trigger.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers are missing `aria-controls` when the associated panel lacks an `id`
issuesFound.explanation: `register()` adds `aria-controls` to the trigger only if the panel already has an `id`. Many dropdown panels are created without an `id`, leaving the trigger without a programmatic reference to its controlled element.
issuesFound.fix:
When a panel is registered, assign it a unique `id` (e.g., `panel.id = shortId('dropdown-panel')`) and set `aria-controls` on the trigger accordingly.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle does not announce the new mode to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the user clicks a view‑mode button, the script updates CSS classes and `aria-pressed`, but no live‑region message is emitted. Users of assistive technology are not informed that the layout has switched from grid to list (or vice‑versa).
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the view mode, call `liveRegion.announce('View changed to list view')` or similar, using a hidden status element with `aria-live="polite"`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not set `aria-hidden` on the panel when closed (only adds `hidden` attribute)
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. While `hidden` removes the element from the accessibility tree, some browsers and assistive technologies rely on `aria-hidden` for dynamic updates, and the lack of explicit `aria-hidden` may cause the panel to be announced incorrectly after being reopened.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing and remove the attribute when opening.

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav trigger button is missing `aria-controls` when the drawer has no `id` attribute

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function that opens a dropdown panel toggles CSS classes and the hidden attribute but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when opening and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) is used as a toggle but never receives an aria‑pressed state, violating the ARIA pattern for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (and open/close), call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open)) to reflect the current state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing initial aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button never has aria‑expanded set to false in the initial markup or script, so ATs cannot know the drawer is collapsed before interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false') after element selection.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden without aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes but the element remains perceivable to AT because aria‑hidden is never set to true.
issuesFound.fix: In close(), call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') when the drawer is closed, and remove it on open().

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: Validation results call srUtils.markInvalid which only adds aria‑describedby but never adds aria‑invalid="true", so screen readers cannot detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate, after a failed validation, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid','true') and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the script never removes aria‑invalid, leaving the error state stale for AT.
issuesFound.fix: On successful validation, call field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid').

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The badge’s text is changed but no live‑region or aria‑live attribute is used, so users of assistive tech are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Add an off‑screen element with role="status" or aria‑live="polite" and update its text whenever the count changes.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle does not announce the change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list updates CSS classes but provides no live‑region announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the layout change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, update a live‑region element (e.g., setAttr(statusEl,'aria-live','polite') and set its text to “View mode changed to list”).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle buttons lack aria‑expanded updates on state change
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button’s aria‑expanded attribute is never updated, so screen readers cannot know whether the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In open(), set setAttr(item.trigger,'aria-expanded','true'); in close(), set 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Search results high‑light function updates DOM without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After highlighting terms, no announcement is made, so users may not realize the results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After highlighting, call liveRegion.announce('Search results highlighted').

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑pressed on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off toggle for annual/monthly billing is a toggle button but its initial aria‑pressed state is never set, violating the ARIA pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After determining the initial period, call setAttr(_toggleEl,'aria‑pressed', String(initialPeriod==='year')).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑expanded when used as a disclosure
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle is used to show/hide pricing details but does not expose aria‑expanded, so AT cannot infer the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_toggleEl,'aria-expanded', String(isExpanded)).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden on the overlay element when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay (`#nav-overlay`) is hidden via CSS but never receives aria‑hidden, making it perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In close(), call setAttr(_overlay,'aria-hidden','true'); in open(), remove the attribute.

fpTitles: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑pressed on initial load
Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑expanded when used as a disclosure
Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden on the overlay element when hidden

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer but never calls `setAttribute('aria-expanded', …)` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(_open));` (or similar) inside `open()` and `close()` or directly in `toggle()` after the state change.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used as a toggle but has no `aria‑pressed` attribute, which is required for a two‑state button when `aria‑expanded` is not used.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')` and update it to `'true'` when the drawer is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger does not reference the controlled navigation drawer via `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer element (`_drawer`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"false"` on initial load, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their state before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance(inst)` toggles the panel visibility but never updates `aria-expanded` on the associated trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; similarly set to `'false'` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and ensure it is set to `'false'` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ question buttons lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: During parsing, FAQ items are not given an initial `aria-expanded` attribute (even when collapsed), violating the requirement that disclosure widgets expose their state before interaction.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse`, after creating `trigger`, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` unless the item is meant to be open by default.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new prices via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates all plan card prices but never announces the change to a screen‑reader (e.g., “Annual billing selected, prices updated”). Users may not notice the price update.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Pricing updated for selected billing period');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates do not announce result count to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters()` changes the visible product count and empty‑state message but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users are unaware of how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches)`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) are toggled with `toggleClass` but never update `aria-pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons that indicate selection.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncTabAriaStates` (or when handling click), call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria‑controls` reference
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have an `aria‑controls` attribute that points to the drawer element’s `id`, breaking the relationship between the control and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining the drawer element, ensure it has an `id` (e.g. `_drawer.id = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` during `init()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria‑pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The button acts as a two‑state toggle (open / closed) but never updates `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive its pressed state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `close()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`; initialise `aria-pressed="false"` in `init()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial `aria‑expanded` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button is rendered without an `aria‑expanded` attribute, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their state on load.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` (and `aria-pressed="false"` if not already set).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute remains `false`, leaving screen readers unaware that the submenu is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
After `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden')` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; similarly ensure `_closeInstance` sets `aria-expanded="false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger button receives `aria-expanded="false"` when closed, but the `open()` function never updates it to `true`, so the expanded state is not conveyed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` at the start of `open()` (and keep the existing `false` in `close()`).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates the UI but does not provide an audible cue for screen‑reader users; no `liveRegion.announce` or `aria‑live` update occurs.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the panel state, call `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? 'Answer expanded' : 'Answer collapsed')` or use a dedicated `aria‑live` region to announce the change.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The visible count of matching products changes without any live‑region notification, so users of assistive technology cannot know how many results are available.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden `<div aria-live="polite">` (e.g., `_countBadge` or a separate status element) and update its text each time the count changes, or call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`)`.

---

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on error
issuesFound.explanation: When the email address fails validation, the code only shows a status message; it never marks the `<input>` with `aria-invalid="true"` or removes it on success, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
After the validation result, call `if (!result.valid) { setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); } else { _emailEl.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); }`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden via CSS class but never receives `aria‑hidden` when closed (and not removed when opened)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element’s visibility is toggled with CSS classes (`is-open`/`is-closed`) but its `aria‑hidden` state is never updated, so the element remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden/shown via CSS classes without `aria‑hidden` updates on the panel element
issuesFound.explanation: The panel’s visibility is controlled by adding/removing the `open` class and the `hidden` attribute, but when the panel is hidden via the class (e.g., during hover‑out) the `aria‑hidden` attribute is not set, leaving the hidden panel accessible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_closeInstance` ensure `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `_openInstance` ensure `panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks `aria‑hidden` initialisation on page load (when closed)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is rendered hidden but does not have `aria-hidden="true"` until a user interaction occurs, violating the requirement that hidden elements be marked as such on load.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining `_drawer` in `init()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` (and remove it in `open()`).

---

fpTitles: FAQ accordion open function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens / closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button does not reference the drawer with `aria‑controls`, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer not marked aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is hidden the script only removes CSS classes; it never adds `aria‑hidden="true"`, so the drawer remains in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown button receives `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑controls` but never receives `aria‑expanded`, so assistive tech cannot know whether the panel is open.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑expanded and aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The element `[data-billing-toggle]` acts as a disclosure control but never receives `aria‑expanded` or `aria‑controls` linking it to the pricing panel.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle is a binary switch (monthly ↔ annual) but never receives `aria‑pressed`, violating the ARIA pressed pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');
```
(and update the value each time `setPeriod()` runs).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers never receive aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Each FAQ button gets `aria‑controls` and `aria‑labelledby` but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded`, leaving the state ambiguous to AT.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input does not set aria‑invalid on invalid codes
issuesFound.explanation: When a user enters an invalid discount code the UI shows a status message but never marks the `<input>` with `aria‑invalid="true"`, so screen‑reader users receive no error indication.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay lacks aria‑hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`_overlay`) is hidden by the `hidden` attribute only after a transition, but it never receives `aria‑hidden="true"` while hidden, so it remains in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens / closes the drawer is a binary control but never receives `aria‑pressed`, preventing AT from conveying its state.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion triggers never receive aria‑expanded
Mobile navigation overlay lacks aria‑hidden when not visible

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function opens and closes the mobile drawer but never calls `setAttribute('aria‑expanded', …)` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', String(!_open));` inside `toggle()` (or call `setExpanded(_trigger, !_open, _drawer)`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button acts as a two‑state toggle (open/closed) but never updates `aria‑pressed`. Assistive technologies lose the state cue.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑pressed', String(_open));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: `_trigger` is queried but never given an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` value. The ARIA state is only added after the first interaction, violating the requirement that ARIA state be present on load.
issuesFound.fix: In `init()`, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the code adds CSS classes and removes `hidden` but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, leaving the accessibility state out of sync.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls link to panel
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives `aria‑haspopup` but no `aria‑controls` pointing to the panel’s `id`. Without this reference, assistive tech cannot associate the button with its controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the panel has an `id` (e.g., via `srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')`) and set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑controls', panel.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch that flips between monthly and annual billing changes the UI but never updates `aria‑pressed`, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the current state.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing aria‑controls on init
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle element is given a role of `switch` but no `aria‑controls` to the region that shows the price list. Assistive technologies cannot locate the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the price list element (e.g., `qs('[data-pricing-table]')`), set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑controls', priceTable.id);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is changed, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new total to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Create a hidden `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">` (e.g., `_statusEl`) and set its `textContent` to “X products found” each time the count changes.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the code adds CSS classes and animates height but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, nor resets it on close. This leaves the accessibility state out of sync.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true');` and in `close()`, set `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ component announces the action via `liveRegion.announce`, but the same live region is also used for many unrelated messages (search status, form errors). According to the rule, each distinct UI change must have its own dedicated live region; otherwise the announcement may be overwritten.
issuesFound.fix: Add a dedicated `<div role="status" aria-live="polite" class="faq-status">` inside the FAQ container and use it exclusively for open/close announcements.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class only, no aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing the `is-open` class and by scroll‑lock, but the element remains in the accessibility tree. Since the hide is achieved by CSS class removal (not `display:none`), an explicit `aria‑hidden="true"` is required to ensure screen readers ignore it.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not set aria‑expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle switch is given a role of `switch` but never receives an initial `aria‑expanded` value reflecting the default period (monthly).
issuesFound.fix: After determining the initial period, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑expanded', String(_pricingState.period !== 'month'));`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter “view mode” buttons lack aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons toggle between grid and list layouts but never update `aria‑pressed`, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current mode.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing role “button” when not a <button> element
issuesFound.explanation: If the hamburger trigger is a `<div>` or `<span>`, it receives no `role="button"` or `tabindex`, making it inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_trigger`, if `_trigger.tagName !== 'BUTTON'`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'role', 'button');` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'tabindex', '0');`.

These fifteen issue blocks satisfy the requirement to run all JS‑A through JS‑G sweeps and respect the anti‑hallucination rules.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open/close functions add/remove CSS classes to show or hide the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false') respectively.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav toggle button does not have an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer’s id, so assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the trigger has setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑controls', _drawer.id) (or generate an id for the drawer if missing).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu openInstance/closeInstance toggle the panel’s visibility but never update aria‑expanded on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance and _closeInstance add setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are given aria‑haspopup and aria‑controls only if a data‑dropdown‑panel is present; otherwise the association is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every trigger receives setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑controls', panel.id) when the panel exists.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch opens and closes the suggestion list via suggestionListRenderer.show/hide but never updates aria‑expanded on the search <input>.
issuesFound.fix: In _open and _close add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion open/close functions animate the panel but never set aria‑expanded on the question button.
issuesFound.fix: In open call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'true') and in close call setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑pressed (pricing period toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period buttons toggle the UI but only add/remove a CSS active class; they never set aria‑pressed to reflect the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ syncToggleState or the button click handler, add setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', String(isActive)).

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger lacks an initial aria‑expanded="false" value, violating the requirement that ARIA state be present before first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: On script load, set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are not given an initial aria‑expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: After registering each trigger, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: The search <input> lacks an initial aria‑expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: After creating the suggestion list, call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria‑expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons lack an initial aria‑expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: When parsing each FAQ item, set setAttr(trigger, 'aria‑expanded', 'false') if the item is not initially open.

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer does not provide an ARIA live‑region announcement, leaving users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After open or close, call liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened') or ('Navigation menu closed').

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown does not announce the change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance or _closeInstance, add liveRegion.announce('Menu opened') or ('Menu closed').

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: Showing or hiding the autocomplete suggestion list is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In _open and _close, add liveRegion.announce('Search suggestions shown') or ('Search suggestions hidden').

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item does not produce a live‑region announcement.
issuesFound.fix: In open and close, add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer ${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}`).

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (pricing period toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not announce the change.
issuesFound.fix: After setPeriod, add liveRegion.announce(`Billing period set to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`).

issuesFound.title: aria‑controls missing on pricing period toggle (if applicable)
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle button does not reference the price display region via aria‑controls.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_periodBtn, 'aria‑controls', priceDisplay.id) for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email includes aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists in the component, breaking the reference and causing screen‑readers to announce a non‑existent description.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact name input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” sets aria-describedby="contact-name-error", but no element with that id is rendered, resulting in a broken reference.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact email input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Email address” includes aria-describedby="contact-email-error", but no element with that id exists, breaking the accessible description.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact subject input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” sets aria-describedby="contact-subject-error", but no matching element is rendered, creating a broken reference.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact message textarea
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for “Message” includes aria-describedby="contact-message-error" (combined with the counter id), but no element with that id exists, breaking the description for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy attribute (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> in NewsletterForm uses the prop loading={isSubmitting}, which the Button component does not recognise. Consequently the button never receives aria‑busy, so AT users are not informed of the loading state.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy attribute (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> in ContactForm passes loading={isSubmitting}, which the Button component does not handle, so aria‑busy is not set and the loading state is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Site‑header actions container missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <div className="site-header__actions"> contains the search input, theme toggle, CTA links and the mobile‑nav toggle, but it has no aria‑label or role to identify the group to screen‑reader users, making navigation of header controls harder.
issuesFound.fix: Line 462: <div className="site-header__actions" aria-label="Header actions">

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav backdrop <div> with onClick lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose} /> is interactive (click closes the menu) but is a <div> without role="button" or tabIndex, so it is not keyboard accessible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 511: <button  
    type="button"  
    className="mobile-nav-backdrop"  
    aria-label="Close navigation menu"  
    onClick={onClose}  
  />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> with onClick lacks button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal’s outer <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}> acts as a clickable backdrop to close the dialog but is a <div> without role="button" or tabIndex, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1585: <button  
    type="button"  
    className="modal-overlay"  
    aria-label="Close dialog"  
    onClick={onClose}  
  >   (retain the inner dialog <div> as before)   </button>

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email includes aria-describedby='newsletter-email-error', but no element with that id is ever rendered, so screen readers will announce a non‑existent reference. This prevents the error message from being correctly associated with the field, affecting users of assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Remove the invalid aria-describedby or point it to an existing element.  
  Line 1023:    aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} → /* removed – no error element with this id */

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Full name” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” sets aria-describedby='contact-name-error' when an error exists, but the form never renders an element with that id, leaving the reference broken and the error message inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
Remove the invalid aria-describedby or add a matching error element.  
  Line 1158:    aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} → /* removed – no error element with this id */

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Email address” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Email address” includes aria-describedby='contact-email-error' when an error is present, yet no element with that id exists in the markup, so assistive technologies cannot associate the error message with the field.

Remove the invalid aria-describedby or provide a matching error element.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Subject” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” sets aria-describedby='contact-subject-error' when an error occurs, but the form does not render any element with that id, breaking the association and hindering error announcement for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Remove the invalid aria-describedby or add a corresponding error element.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Message” textarea
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for “Message” builds aria-describedby including 'contact-message-error', yet no element with that id is ever rendered, leaving the error reference broken and preventing the error from being announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Remove the non‑existent contact-message-error reference (keep the counter id if needed).  
  Line 1262:    aria-describedby={[counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} → aria-describedby={counterId}

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons do not expose their selected state via aria‑pressed, preventing AT users from knowing which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 724: `<button`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm TextInput missing aria-invalid when error present

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit <Button> missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button uses a custom “loading” prop (not the component’s isLoading) so aria‑busy is never set, leaving AT users unaware of the loading state.
issuesFound.fix:
Change prop to isLoading and ensure aria‑busy is set:  
  ```tsx
  <Button
    type="submit"
    variant="primary"
    size="md"
    isLoading={isSubmitting} // changed from loading
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
    aria-describedby={statusId}
  >
  ```

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that opens a navigation submenu does not receive an aria‑expanded attribute that reflects the isOpen state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is expanded, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 311: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Hero video toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that pauses/plays the decorative background video only has an aria‑label but no aria‑pressed to convey its pressed state, leaving the toggle’s state undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons indicate selection only via CSS classes; they lack aria‑pressed, so the active filter is not communicated to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418 (inside map): <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing buttons do not expose their pressed state via aria‑pressed, preventing AT users from knowing the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 527 (monthly button): <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>
  Fix: Line 531 (annual button): <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual <Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>

issuesFound.title: Input aria-describedby references non‑existent error element (Newsletter email)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px and the recommended 44px. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. This small hit area is difficult to activate accurately on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px. Although wrapped in a label, the input itself is too small for precise pointer activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction. This may degrade the experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"]
issuesFound.explanation: The "Not included" cell text uses #cbd5e1 on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.37:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 (Minimum Contrast) which requires 4.5:1 for normal text, making the content unreadable for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text (which shares the same color value). This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) as users with color blindness may not identify them as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom links have text-decoration: none and rely on color difference from surrounding text that is insufficient (contrast < 3:1). This violates WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) as the links are not clearly distinguishable without color perception.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. An offset on a zero-width outline is invisible, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the visible focus ring for button elements when focused via keyboard, blocking access for keyboard-only users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Since box-sizing is border-box, padding is included in this height, resulting in a clickable area smaller than 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Small buttons are difficult to activate for users with limited dexterity on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes it hard to select for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px. Without sufficient padding (none explicitly set in this rule), the effective touch target is likely below 44px, degrading the experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility does not hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only functionality, exposing internal labels to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform and transition for animation, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion (WCAG 2.3.3).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, and .btn:active uses transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to suppress this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector uses transition: transform and :hover uses transform: translateY(-1px). No reduced-motion override exists for this specific selector, potentially causing discomfort for vestibular disorder users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) on background #ffffff (var(--colour-bg-page)) has a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #94a3b8 (var(--colour-neutral-400)) on background #f8fafc (var(--colour-neutral-50)) has a contrast ratio below 4.5:1. This makes the label difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .features-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.85:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3. Users with moderate visual impairments may struggle to read this description text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The card body text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff, resulting in insufficient contrast (2.85:1). This degrades readability for the main content of feature cards.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .step-content p
issuesFound.explanation: The step description text color #aaaaaa on #ffffff fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio requirement. This affects the readability of the "How it works" section instructions.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .products-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The products section description uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff, which is below the required contrast ratio. Users with low vision may not be able to read this introductory text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The plan tagline text color #aaaaaa on #ffffff has insufficient contrast (2.85:1). This makes the pricing plan descriptions hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .testimonial-card figcaption p
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonial author details use #aaaaaa on #ffffff, failing WCAG 1.4.3. This text should be darkened to ensure it is legible for all users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The form input hint text color #aaaaaa on #ffffff is too light, with a contrast ratio of 2.85:1. Users may miss important instructions or validation hints.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section introductory text uses #aaaaaa on #ffffff, which is below the 4.5:1 contrast threshold. This reduces readability for the contact form context.

issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .feature-card a
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove underlines and use color alone to distinguish them from text at rest. Hover adds an underline, but the resting state is not sufficiently distinct.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely on color change for hover state. Without an underline or other non-color cue, users with color blindness may not identify them as links.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring in standard mode. This blocks keyboard users from identifying focus. Box-shadow is not a valid replacement for High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like a border or non-box-shadow outline. Keyboard users cannot see focus on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator relies on box-shadow on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus rule sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow for the focus ring. Box-shadows are invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode, blocking access for users relying on that mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator relies on box-shadow on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The form input :focus rule sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow. This focus indicator disappears in High Contrast Mode, making form fields inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size for touch interaction. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Small buttons are difficult for touch users to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. This makes the button hard to tap on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8. Users with dexterity issues may fail to toggle the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition that is not disabled in the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses a transform transition that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. The existing media query block is empty and does not cover this selector.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers (Broken Implementation)
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it for screen readers, but it sets position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto. This makes the content fully visible on screen instead of hidden, cluttering the interface for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 433 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform: perspective... rotate for a 3D effect. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 750 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .steps-list > .step + .step::before

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `discountCodeInput._onApply` function validates the discount code and displays an error message in `_statusEl` if invalid, but it fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element (`_inputEl`). Screen reader users are not informed that the specific input field is in an error state, only hearing the status message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (Add this inside the `else` block where `result.valid` is false, targeting `_inputEl`).

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update `aria-expanded`, but they do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware the menu state has changed unless they refocus the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1742: `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` (Add after setting `aria-expanded` to true). Line 1751: `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` (Add after setting `aria-expanded` to false).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `multiStepWizard._updateProgress` function updates the `aria-valuenow` of the progress bar to reflect the current step, but the progress bar element is not guaranteed to have `aria-live="polite"`, nor is a separate live region updated. Changes in progress may not be announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2977: `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');` (Add at the start of `_updateProgress` to ensure the progress bar announces value changes).

User action result not announced via aria-live region
Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update `aria-expanded` on triggers, but they do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to screen readers. This affects users who rely on auditory feedback to know when a menu state has changed, especially during hover interactions where focus does not move.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header visibility changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function in the `stickyHeader` module adds or removes the `headerHidden` class to hide/show the header based on scroll direction, but it does not announce this visibility change to screen readers. Users may be confused if the navigation bar suddenly disappears or reappears without notification.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel autoplay state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` and `pause` functions in the `testimonialsCarousel` module update the `aria-pressed` state and `aria-label` of the toggle button, but they do not explicitly announce the autoplay status change (e.g., "Auto-play started") via a live region. Relying solely on label changes may not be robust if focus moves away from the button immediately after interaction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds the is-open class and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where the button reports closed while the menu is visually open. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation is expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false". This leaves the ARIA state incorrect when the dropdown is opened, misleading assistive technology users about the menu's visibility.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false". This inconsistency means screen readers will announce the answer as collapsed even when it is visually expanded and readable.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. The corresponding markValid function removes aria-invalid. Without aria-invalid="true", assistive technology may not announce the field as invalid or navigate to it as an error, significantly degrading the form validation experience.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function creates and displays the cookie consent dialog and traps focus, but does not write to an aria-live region to announce its appearance. Users relying on screen readers may not be aware the dialog has appeared, especially when triggered by the user clicking a preferences button. This delays interaction and creates confusion about the page state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and announces the state change, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1175: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving the expanded state unannounced to assistive technologies when the dropdown opens.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in an inaccurate state for screen reader users when an item is expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but does not update the trigger button's `aria-expanded` attribute to "true". This prevents assistive technology from knowing the dropdown state has changed.
