issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds an error description but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Assistive technologies rely on this attribute to announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function missing aria-invalid removal
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as valid, the function removes the error description but fails to remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute. The field may remain announced as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1103: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge (_countBadge) is updated dynamically when filters are applied, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users are unaware that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1858: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle visual active state via CSS class but do not update aria-pressed or aria-selected. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1973: btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing module price updates missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, price values are updated dynamically in the DOM without an accompanying live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2178: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: viewportReveal elements missing aria-hidden management
issuesFound.explanation: Elements hidden initially via CSS (e.g. opacity: 0) and revealed via class addition remain in the accessibility tree before they are visible. They should be hidden from assistive technology until revealed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2868: el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line 2888: el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute on the overlay, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen readers will not announce the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1670: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 1688: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialisation does not set aria-expanded or aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function locates the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` or `aria-controls` attributes on the trigger. Assistive technologies cannot identify the relationship or initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1737: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` functions manage the panel's `hidden` attribute but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger (`inst.trigger`). The button state remains static to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1857: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 1872: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger (`item.trigger`). The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, causing a state mismatch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2573: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3390: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the error description but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` (or remove the attribute). The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3404: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function announces "Navigation menu opened", but `mobileNav.close()` does not announce "Navigation menu closed". Screen reader users are not notified when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1688: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function lacks live region announcement

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function lacks live region announcement

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters lacks live region announcement

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 (AA). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating such a small control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1414: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;` or increase the clickable area using a larger transparent label or padding while keeping the visual checkbox at 20px if design requires.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets a min-height of 36px (24–43px range) with only 6px vertical padding, failing to meet the 44×44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.5 AAA). The cumulative touch area may be smaller than ideal for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 607: `min-height: 44px;` or add compensating padding/margin to ensure a 44px touch area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height/width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28×28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient compensation to reach the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.5 AAA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 749: `width: 44px; height: 44px;` or increase the dimensions to meet the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set a min-height of exactly 24px with no compensating padding, meeting the minimum 24px requirement but falling short of the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.5 AAA) without additional spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1424: `min-height: 44px;` or add `padding-block: 10px;` to achieve a 44px touch area.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes `transform` in its transition property (line 542) and applies a translateY transform on :active (line 550), creating spatial movement. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule overrides this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses `transition: transform var(--transition-base)` for its slide-in animation. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unnecessary movement when this element receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes `transform` in its transition property, enabling spatial movement on interaction. This is not covered by the existing reduced-motion media queries which target other specific components.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero secondary button includes `transform` in its transition property, enabling spatial movement on interaction. This is not covered by the existing reduced-motion media queries.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies a translateY and scale transform on hover that is not overridden in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block, which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover class.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The search input placeholder uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.4:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 721: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);` (or darker) to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast against the #f8fafc background.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links (#nav-menu > li > a) have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color changes (to --colour-brand-primary) on hover to indicate interactivity, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color). No non-colour visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 674 (add): `text-decoration: underline;` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to the hover state, or use `font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold);` on hover to provide a non-colour cue.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px as a replacement. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring; it merely offsets an outline that has zero width. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will see no focus indicator on any interactive element unless overridden by more specific rules.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. In forced-colors mode, box-shadow (used elsewhere as a replacement) is suppressed by the OS, making outline the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it entirely leaves keyboard users with no visible focus in high-contrast themes.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. More critically, it lacks the absolute positioning and clipping necessary to keep content in the accessibility tree while hiding it visually. This implementation actually renders content visible and in-flow, or if combined with other rules, may remove it from assistive technologies entirely. The correct sr-only pattern requires position: absolute, 1px×1px dimensions, and clip properties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 461: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)). This causes spatial movement on state changes (e.g., :active transform) that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule targets this selector to disable the transform transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition, causing the button to animate spatially on hover/focus. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users sensitive to motion.

Line 886: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); min-height: 32px; border-radius: var(--radius-xl); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa on background-color: var(--colour-bg-subtle) which is #f8fafc. The contrast ratio is 2.08:1, failing the 3:1 requirement for large text (18px) and the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1147: .contact-section > p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-align: center; max-width: 560px; margin-inline: auto; margin-bottom: var(--space-10); padding-inline: var(--space-6); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change (from var(--colour-neutral-700) to var(--colour-brand-primary)) on hover. Users with color vision deficiencies cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text or identify when they are hovered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 647: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines and only change color on hover (from var(--colour-neutral-400) to var(--colour-neutral-0)), providing no non-colour visual indicator for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1272: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines and rely solely on color change (from var(--colour-neutral-400) to var(--colour-neutral-0)) on hover, failing to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1283: .social-links a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible

Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section > p

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels misuse ARIA tab role
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard step panels are assigned role="tabpanel" (line: step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel')), but the navigation buttons used to switch steps are standard buttons, not elements with role="tab". This violates the ARIA tab pattern which requires tabpanels to be associated with tab elements. Screen readers may not correctly convey the relationship between the navigation buttons and the content panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2894: Remove `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` and use a more appropriate role such as "region" with an aria-labelledby pointing to the step title, or remove the role entirely if the sections are properly marked up with headings.

issuesFound.title: Inline error messages have conflicting ARIA live region attributes
issuesFound.explanation: Error message elements are created with both role="alert" and aria-live="polite". The alert role implies aria-live="assertive" and aria-atomic="true", which conflicts with the polite live region setting. This can cause unpredictable announcement behavior in screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2764: Remove the aria-live attribute and keep only role="alert", or change role to "status" if the message should be polite. Corrected code: `const errorEl = createElement('span', { id: existingId, class: 'field-error', role: 'alert' });`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer implements focus trapping (via createFocusTrap) and prevents interaction with the rest of the page, behaving as a modal dialog. However, the drawer element (_drawer) is not marked with aria-modal="true", so screen readers may not announce it as a modal or prevent virtual cursor from escaping the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1923: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` immediately after opening the drawer, or ensure the drawer element in the HTML has aria-modal="true".

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons use conflicting ARIA states
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are assigned both aria-pressed and aria-selected attributes simultaneously. These attributes have different semantics; aria-pressed indicates a toggle button state, while aria-selected indicates a selected option in a list. Using both creates confusion about the control type.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2537-2538: Remove one of the attributes. For a set of mutually exclusive options, use only aria-selected (and role="tab" or role="radio"). For toggle buttons, use only aria-pressed. Corrected code: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` (remove the aria-selected line).

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs incorrectly use aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons use aria-pressed to indicate the active filter state. However, these are mutually exclusive options functioning as a tab list or radio group, not as toggle buttons. The aria-selected attribute is more appropriate for indicating the selected state in such components.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2374: Change `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` to `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` and ensure the buttons have role="tab" or the container has role="tablist".

issuesFound.title: Search clear button lacks ensured accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button is selected from the DOM (qs(cfg.clearSelector)), but the JavaScript does not ensure it has an accessible name via aria-label or text content. If the HTML contains only an icon (e.g., an "X" symbol without text), screen reader users will not know the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2244: After selecting the button, add an aria-label if missing: `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_clearBtn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search'); }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-haspopup
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls a menu (drawer) but the JavaScript only ensures aria-expanded and aria-controls attributes. It does not ensure aria-haspopup="true" or aria-haspopup="menu", which is needed to indicate to screen readers that the button opens a menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1948: Add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-haspopup')) { setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true'); }` alongside the aria-expanded check.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons lack ensured accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons are selected via `[data-theme-toggle]` attribute, but the JavaScript does not verify or ensure they have accessible names (aria-label or text content). Icon-only theme buttons would be inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3284: Add a check to ensure accessible names: `if (!btn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !btn.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.themeToggle || 'Toggle theme'); }`

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle buttons lack ensured accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) are updated with aria-pressed state, but the JavaScript does not ensure they have accessible names. If the HTML uses only icons, screen readers will announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2403: Before setting aria-pressed, ensure the button has an accessible name: `if (!b.getAttribute('aria-label') && !b.textContent.trim()) { setAttr(b, 'aria-label', mode === 'grid' ? 'Grid view' : 'List view'); }`

issuesFound.title: Wizard navigation buttons lack ensured accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard previous, next, and submit buttons are selected from the DOM, but the JavaScript does not ensure they have accessible names. While the HTML likely contains text, the initialization should verify this for robustness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2856: Add checks for each button to ensure aria-label or text content is present, or set default labels: `if (_prevBtn && !_prevBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_prevBtn, 'aria-label', 'Previous step');` etc.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-describedby linking to instructions
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone is given role="button" and an aria-label, but if there are file type or size restrictions, they are not programmatically associated with the zone via aria-describedby. Users may not know the constraints until after attempting to upload.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3018: If instructions exist in the DOM, link them: `const descId = zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-desc'); if (descId) setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-describedby', descId);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table not programmatically associated with accessibility helper
issuesFound.explanation: The plan comparison table is highlighted visually on hover, but the JavaScript does not call srUtils.makeTableAccessible to ensure table headers have scope attributes, potentially making the table difficult to navigate for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2643: After selecting the table, add: `if (_tableEl) srUtils.makeTableAccessible(_tableEl);`

issuesFound.title: FAQ search input missing aria-controls initialization check
issuesFound.explanation: While the FAQ search input is given aria-controls pointing to the FAQ list, there is no verification that the target container actually has the corresponding ID, which could result in a broken ARIA reference if the HTML structure differs from expectations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2810: Verify the target exists: `const containerId = srUtils.ensureId(_containerEl, 'faq-list'); setAttr(_searchInputEl, 'aria-controls', containerId);` (ensureId is already used, so this is actually correct, but the check for existence of the element could be added).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close on outside click does not announce closure
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed by clicking outside, the _closeAll function is called without returnFocus parameter, meaning focus is not returned to the trigger and no announcement is made. While focus moves to the clicked element, users may not realize the dropdown has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2094: Add a live region announcement when closing via outside click: `if (_instances.some(i => i.open)) { liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); }` before calling _closeAll().

issuesFound.title: Character counter live region may be overly verbose
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter uses aria-live="polite" and aria-atomic="true", updating on every keystroke (debounced). This can result in excessive interruptions for screen reader users typing in the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2956: Change to aria-live="off" and update only on blur, or use aria-describedby to link the counter to the field instead of using a live region, allowing users to check the count on demand rather than hearing it constantly.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications may stack without user dismissal control
issuesFound.explanation: While toast notifications have dismiss buttons, they auto-dismiss after a timeout (defaultDuration, errorDuration, etc.). There is no mechanism to pause or stop the auto-dismiss timer when the user hovers over or focuses the toast, violating the requirement for user control over time limits.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: Add mouseenter/mouseleave and focusin/focusout handlers to pause/resume the auto-dismiss timer when the user interacts with the toast.

issuesFound.title: Form error summary uses role="alert" which interrupts users
issuesFound.explanation: The form error summary container uses role="alert", which is assertive and will interrupt the screen reader immediately. While errors are important, moving focus to the summary (which happens immediately after) is sufficient; the alert role may cause the error heading to be announced twice (once via focus, once via alert).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: Remove role="alert" and rely on the focus management and aria-labelledby to convey the error presence, or change to role="status" for a less intrusive announcement.

issuesFound.title: Media player shortcut keys not documented
issuesFound.explanation: The media player defines keyboard shortcuts (Space, K, M, Arrows, etc.) but there is no mechanism provided to inform users about these shortcuts, violating the guideline for making functionality predictable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3220: Add a help button or aria-describedby on the media element listing the available shortcuts, or provide a settings menu with keyboard shortcut documentation.

issuesFound.title: Analytics event batching may delay critical announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Errors are batched with a 2-second delay before sending to analytics. While not a direct accessibility barrier, critical JavaScript errors that affect accessibility features could be lost if the user navigates away before the batch sends.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: For accessibility-critical errors, send immediately rather than batching, or ensure the batch is flushed on pagehide events (which is already done, but could be enhanced).

issuesFound.title: Prefetch manager does not respect prefers-reduced-data
issuesFound.explanation: While the prefetch manager checks for navigator.connection.saveData and prefers-reduced-data media query, it does not check for prefers-reduced-motion, which may be correlated with data saving preferences in some user scenarios.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3538: Add check for `prefersReducedMotion()` to the data saver check, as users who prefer reduced motion often also prefer reduced data usage.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form selects an email input element but does not programmatically add the autocomplete="email" attribute. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from automatically filling the user's email address, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4320: Add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');` after selecting the input element in newsletterForm.init.

issuesFound.title: Contact form full name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form binds validation to the full name input (selector '[name="full_name"]') but does not set autocomplete="name". This prevents automatic filling of the user's name, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4205: After `const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl);`, add `if (field && cfg.selector.includes('name')) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');` (or specifically target the full_name field with `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');`).

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form binds validation to the email input (selector '[name="email"]') but does not set autocomplete="email". This prevents automatic filling of the email address, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4206: Add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');` after selecting the email field.

issuesFound.title: Wizard full name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard binds validation to the name input (selector '[name="wizard_name"]') but does not set autocomplete="name". This prevents automatic filling, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4520: Add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'name');` after selecting the wizard_name field.

issuesFound.title: Wizard email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard binds validation to the email input (selector '[name="wizard_email"]') but does not set autocomplete="email". This prevents automatic filling, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4521: Add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');` after selecting the wizard_email field.

issuesFound.title: Wizard cardholder name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard binds validation to the card name input (selector '[name="wizard_card_name"]') but does not set autocomplete="cc-name". This prevents automatic filling of payment information, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4524: Add `setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'cc-name');` after selecting the wizard_card_name field.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard step panels misuse role="tabpanel"
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard assigns role="tabpanel" to step panel elements (step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel')), but there is no corresponding tablist or tab elements controlling these panels. The navigation uses Previous/Next buttons, not tabs. This incorrect role causes screen readers to announce the content as a tab panel when it is not, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4550: Remove `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'tabpanel');` and instead use `step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'region');` or remove the role attribute entirely and rely on the aria-label for the section.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update aria-expanded, but they do not write to an aria-live region to announce the state change to screen reader users. While aria-expanded changes are announced by some AT, the addition of new content (the menu) should be explicitly announced via the liveRegion utility used elsewhere in the codebase, ensuring users are aware the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3850 (in _openInstance after setAttr): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line 3860 (in _closeInstance after setAttr): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Comparison table expandable section missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's setComparisonTableExpanded function manages the expanded state of the comparison table, but the init function does not initialize the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "false" when the page loads (assuming the table starts collapsed). This fails WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 4.1.2 because the initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5750 (in pricing.init): After `highlightPlan(recommended);`, add code to initialize the comparison table trigger: `const compTrigger = qs('[data-expand-comparison]'); if (compTrigger) setAttr(compTrigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-describedby reference to constraints
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField creates an upload zone with role="button" and aria-label, but does not programmatically associate the file type and size constraint information (maxFileSizeMB, allowedMimeTypes) with the input using aria-describedby. Users may not be aware of the restrictions until after attempting to upload.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4800 (in bind function after creating statusEl): Create a description element containing the constraint text (e.g., "Max file size: ${config.maxFileSizeMB}MB. Allowed types: ${config.allowedMimeTypes.join(', ')}"), give it an ID, append it to the zone, and add `setAttr(inputEl, 'aria-describedby', descId);`.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle elements lack keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher module attaches click listeners to elements matching '[data-theme-toggle]' but does not verify they are native <button> elements, nor does it add role="button", tabindex="0", or Enter/Space keydown handlers. If the HTML uses <div> or <span> elements for these toggles, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggles lack keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module delegates click handling to '[data-view-mode]' elements but does not ensure these are native buttons or provide role="button", tabindex="0", or keyboard activation handlers. Keyboard users cannot activate grid/list view toggles if the HTML uses non-button elements.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons lack keyboard activation support
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module uses createRovingTabindex for arrow key navigation between period buttons, but relies on a container click listener for activation. If the HTML uses non-button elements for [data-period], keyboard users can navigate with arrows but cannot activate with Enter/Space because no keydown handlers are attached to the individual items or container for activation keys.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close state not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close (via hover, click, or keyboard), the state change is communicated only through the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger. There is no accompanying live-region announcement to inform screen reader users that the menu panel has opened or closed, which is particularly important when the menu contains many items or when opened via hover (where focus does not move).

fpTitles: Theme switcher toggle elements lack keyboard accessibility
Product filter view mode toggles lack keyboard accessibility
Pricing period toggle buttons lack keyboard activation support
Dropdown menu open/close state not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the button as "collapsed" even when the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2068: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2154: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel and adds CSS classes but omits setting the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry that confuses assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3427: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel opens, the function updates internal state but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the search input element (which has role="combobox"). The _close() function correctly sets it to "false", so screen readers only hear state changes when closing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2894: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, this function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, and the markValid() function only removes the attribute without ever setting it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1314: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function announces "Navigation menu opened" when opening, but provides no corresponding announcement when closing. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has closed, causing confusion about focus context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2089: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns close (via Escape key, outside click, or selecting an item), no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with keyboard may not realize the menu has disappeared and their focus has moved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2169: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While open() announces when an FAQ item expands, close() provides no corresponding announcement when collapsing. This creates an asymmetry where screen reader users are only informed of half the state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3442: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The code declares a `_trap` variable for focus management and deactivates it in close(), but never initializes or activates a focus trap when opening the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the open menu into the background page.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile menu closes, focus is not returned to the hamburger trigger button. Keyboard users are left with focus in an undefined location (likely the bottom of the page where the drawer was).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2089: Add `_trigger.focus();` immediately after `_unlockScroll();` and before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus move to drawer content
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button. For a modal navigation pattern, focus should move to the first focusable element inside the drawer (or the drawer itself) so users can immediately navigate the menu.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing focus return on outside click
issuesFound.explanation: When the dropdown closes via outside click or focus leaving the menu, the function is called without the returnFocus parameter (defaults to false). Focus is lost and does not return to the trigger button, forcing keyboard users to navigate back manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2169: Modify _closeAll() to pass `true` for returnFocus, or modify _onDocumentFocusin and _onDocumentClick to call _closeInstance(inst, true).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls when drawer lacks id
issuesFound.explanation: The code attempts to set aria-controls only if `_drawer.id` exists. If the drawer element in the HTML lacks an id attribute, the trigger button will not have aria-controls, breaking the programmatic relationship between trigger and content for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent accept/decline buttons missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users accept or decline cookies, the banner disappears but no confirmation is announced to screen readers. Users may not realize their choice was registered, especially since focus is lost when the banner is removed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 8245: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieAccepted || 'Cookie preferences saved. You have accepted all cookies.');` before calling _hideBanner().
  Line 8252: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieDeclined || 'Cookie preferences saved. Only essential cookies are active.');` before calling _hideBanner().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function reveals the navigation drawer by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1532: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function in dropdownMenu reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the dropdown is open and that navigation options are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1624: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the FAQ answer panel using animateHeight but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not convey the expanded state of the accordion item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3158: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function declares a focus trap variable (_trap) but never instantiates or activates it. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the background page content while the modal overlay is visible, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function deactivates the focus trap (if it existed) but because the trap was never activated in open(), focus is not returned to the trigger button. Focus is lost when the menu closes, forcing keyboard users to restart navigation from the top of the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1552: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` after `_unlockScroll();` to ensure focus returns to the menu button.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: When auto-play is disabled in configuration, the play/pause button is not initialised with aria-pressed="false", leaving the toggle state ambiguous for screen reader users on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3485: Add `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` before the conditional auto-play check.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (_clearBtn) is selected from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label) if the HTML element lacks visible text or a label. Screen readers will announce it as an unlabeled button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2103: Add `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_clearBtn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels missing ARIA menu roles
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened, the code moves focus to the first item but does not ensure the panel has role="menu" and the items have role="menuitem". Screen readers may not recognize the dropdown as a menu structure.

issuesFound.title: contactForm error summary not cleared on success
issuesFound.explanation: When the contact form submits successfully, the _showSuccess() function does not remove the error summary element from the DOM or hide it. If a previous submission had errors, the error summary remains visible alongside the success message, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3755: Add `_hideErrorSummary();` at the start of the _showSuccess() function.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard step panels missing aria-live for step changes
issuesFound.explanation: While the wizard announces step changes via liveRegion.announce(), the step panel content itself is not marked as a live region. If the step content updates dynamically without moving focus, screen readers may not announce the new content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3840: Add `setAttr(entering.panelEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` and `setAttr(entering.panelEl, 'aria-atomic', 'true');` when initialising step panels.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField progress missing aria-valuetext
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload progress bar uses the native progress element, but when the upload completes or errors, the status is conveyed only via text content change. The progress element should have aria-valuetext updated to convey the status to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3975: Add `progressEl.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', entry.status);` when updating status.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher missing meta theme-color update
issuesFound.explanation: When the theme is switched between light and dark, the code updates the data-theme attribute but does not update the meta[name="theme-color"] tag, which mobile browsers use to color the address bar. This creates a visual inconsistency for users.

issuesFound.title: characterCounter missing aria-describedby association
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter element is created and inserted into the DOM, but it is not programmatically associated with the input field via aria-describedby. Screen reader users will not hear the character count when focusing the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3108: Add `srUtils.addDescribedBy(field, counterEl);` after creating the counter element.

issuesFound.title: skipLinkManager focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The skipLinkManager removes the tabindex attribute from the target element on blur, but if the user navigates away using Shift+Tab (backwards), the blur event fires and removes the tabindex, potentially preventing the user from tabbing back to the target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1308: Remove the blur event listener that clears tabindex, or only remove tabindex after a delay to ensure it persists for backwards navigation.

fpTitles: mobileNav.close() missing focus management
dropdownMenu panels missing ARIA menu roles
contactForm error summary not cleared on success
fileUploadField progress missing aria-valuetext
themeSwitcher missing meta theme-color update
characterCounter missing aria-describedby association
skipLinkManager focus management

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving blind users unaware that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the expanded state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1844: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button when expanding an answer panel. While the close() function sets it to "false", the asymmetry means screen readers never hear the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3124: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility marks fields as invalid by adding error descriptions but omits setting aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users, preventing them from knowing which fields need correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null check at the start of the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() lacks live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen will not know that the menu has closed and that focus has returned to the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1710: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately after the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() lacks live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu changes the visible content but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users are not informed that a menu has appeared and that they can navigate its items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1844: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` immediately after the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() lacks live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes content from view without notifying screen reader users. This silent state change can cause confusion about why keyboard navigation has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1854: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` immediately after the line `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() lacks live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ answer panel does not trigger a live-region announcement. While opening announces "expanded", the lack of a corresponding "collapsed" announcement creates an asymmetrical experience for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3146: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` immediately before the closing brace of the close function.

fpTitles: faqAccordion.close() lacks live-region announcement

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance open and close functions manage the panel's hidden attribute and CSS classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger).
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2550: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in _openInstance.
  Line ~2565: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate the panel height and manage the hidden attribute but do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (item.trigger).
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~4200: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the open function.
  Line ~4220: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies. The HTML contract expects aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3350: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after calculating isActive.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu closes, no announcement is made to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed and focus has moved.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2420: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before emitting the mobilenav:close event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not trigger any announcement to screen readers via the live region system.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2550: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` in _openInstance.
  Line ~2565: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but the updated result count is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3300: After calculating totalMatches, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually and persists to storage but does not announce the period change or new price information to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3550: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid and markValid do not manage aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation utility functions manage aria-describedby for error messages but fail to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1150: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` in markInvalid.
  Line ~1160: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`) in markValid.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger lacks initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load when the menu is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2450: After verifying _trigger and _drawer exist, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger lacks aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's ID, preventing screen reader users from understanding the relationship between the button and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2450: Ensure _drawer has an ID (using srUtils.ensureId) and add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input lacks initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially before the user interacts with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3000: After setting other attributes on the input, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space key handlers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers that are not native button elements have role="button" and tabindex="0" but the keyboard handler only manages Arrow keys, Home, and End. It does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4250: Add cases for `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that prevent default and call `toggle(item)`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button lacks keyboard accessibility safeguards
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected via querySelector but the code assumes it is clickable. If the element is not a native button, it lacks role="button", tabindex="0", and Enter/Space key handlers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Users can tab out of the drawer into the background content while it is visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2400: After opening the drawer, add `const trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); trap.activate();` and store the trap instance to deactivate it on close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close lacks focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, potentially leaving keyboard users at the bottom of the page or in an unknown location.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2420: At the start of the close function, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` before hiding the drawer.

fpTitles: faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space key handlers
scrollToTop button lacks keyboard accessibility safeguards
mobileNav close lacks focus return

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they do not synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons (inst.trigger). This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2987: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 3002: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close functions animate the answer panels and update visual state, but they fail to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger when opening or aria-expanded="false" when closing. Although the attribute is set during initialization, it becomes stale after the first toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4132: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 4160: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid by adding error messages and CSS classes, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the form control. Screen readers rely on this attribute to announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4573: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the invalid block before srUtils.markInvalid.
  Line 4578: Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); inside the valid block after srUtils.markValid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function locates the trigger button and drawer elements but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger when the menu is closed on page load. Additionally, the trigger lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2914: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls, but it does not initialize aria-expanded="false" at page load when the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3532: Add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the product grid, count badge, and empty state visibility based on filter criteria, but it does not announce the result count or filter changes to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3767: After _updateCountBadge(totalMatches); add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products match your filters`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates all plan cards with new prices and billing periods, but it does not announce the billing period change or price updates to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4317: After updating the prices, add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips._onChipClick function removes filter chips and updates the filter state, but it does not announce the removal action or the updated filter state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3880: After the switch statement handling removal, add liveRegion.announce(`Filter removed: ${label}`);

issuesFound.title: File upload busy state not cleared
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField._uploadEntry function sets srUtils.setBusy(binding.statusEl, true) when an upload begins, but it does not clear the aria-busy attribute (set to false) when the upload completes or errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5045: Add srUtils.setBusy(binding.statusEl, false); in the load handler.
  Line 5055: Add srUtils.setBusy(binding.statusEl, false); in the error handler.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons may lack keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module delegates click handling to elements matching '[data-filter-value]'. If these elements are not native <button> elements, they will not be keyboard accessible (cannot be activated with Enter or Space) because the code does not add role="button" or keydown handlers for activation.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the HTML uses <button> elements for filter tabs, or add role="button", tabindex="0", and keydown handlers for Enter/Space in the delegate setup.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons may lack keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module delegates click handling to elements matching '[data-period]'. If these are not native buttons, they lack keyboard accessibility as the code does not ensure they are focusable or handle Enter/Space activation.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the HTML uses <button> elements for period selection, or add role="button", tabindex="0", and Enter/Space keydown handling.

Pricing period buttons may lack keyboard accessibility

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria-expanded="false"

In the else block (result.valid is false), after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`:

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not given an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute when the module initializes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3590 (in init function, after _initialised = true): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation menu changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2428 (in open function, before emit): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation menu is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2458 (in close function, before emit): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not announce the action to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2593 (in _openInstance function, after setAttr for aria-expanded): liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2618 (in _closeInstance function, after setAttr for aria-expanded): liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates visible prices throughout the page but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4155 (in setPeriod function, after _syncToggleState(period)): liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding a FAQ item reveals the answer content but does not announce the action or the item title to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4770 (in open function, after emit): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title) || `${item.title} — answer expanded`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing a FAQ item is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4795 (in close function, after emit): liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title) || `${item.title} — answer collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and result count but does not announce the filter results or count to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3780 (in applyFilters function, inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _syncPagination): liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but fails to update aria-expanded on the trigger button. The setExpanded helper exists in the codebase but is not used in these functions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2258: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _openInstance(). Line 2272: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close functions do not update aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (role="combobox") with an expandable suggestion list, but the aria-expanded attribute is never toggled to reflect the open/closed state of the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2855: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _open(). Line 2865: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _close().

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function marks the field invalid visually and links the error message via aria-describedby, but does not set aria-invalid="true". Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3358: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` in the else block where srUtils.markInvalid is called.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, the error state is cleared visually and aria-describedby is removed, but aria-invalid is not reset to "false" or removed. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3352: Add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` in the if block where srUtils.markValid is called.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads and the menu is closed. The attribute must be present from the start to indicate the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2095: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after confirming _trigger and _drawer exist.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but fails to initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2235: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded on combobox input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the page loads with the suggestion list closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2915: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after _inputEl is assigned.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3575: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick does not announce scroll action
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button moves the viewport and focus, but does not announce the action completion to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2465: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after the scroll command.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close do not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu changes the UI significantly but does not announce "Navigation menu opened" or "Navigation menu closed" via the live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2112: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` in open(). Line 2132: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in close().

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance do not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` in _openInstance(). Line 2275: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` in _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close do not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse but the state change is not announced via the live region, making it unclear to screen reader users which item opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3920: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title));` in open(). Line 3940: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title));` in close().

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips _onChipClick does not announce filter removal
issuesFound.explanation: When users remove a filter chip, the product grid updates but the removal action and resulting filter state are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3215: Add `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed');` after the switch statement that handles the removal.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is a modal interface that traps focus when open, but the focus trap is never activated in the open() function despite the _trap variable being declared and deactivated in close().
issuesFound.fix: Line 2110: Add `_trap.activate();` after opening the drawer and before emitting the event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls set to reference the drawer element, failing to establish the programmatic relationship required by the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2096: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` after confirming both elements exist.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-haspopup on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger acts as a menu button but lacks aria-haspopup to indicate it controls a menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2097: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.HASPOPUP, 'true');` after setting aria-controls.

fpTitles: faqAccordion open/close do not announce state change
activeFilterChips _onChipClick does not announce filter removal
mobileNav init missing aria-haspopup on trigger

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput element has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when validation fails, but FormGroup is not rendering an ErrorMessage element with that id because the errorId prop is not passed. Additionally, FormGroup expects the error text via the errorMessage prop, but NewsletterForm passes it as error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145-1148: Change to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when validation fails, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with that id because errorId is not passed, and the error message is passed via the wrong prop name (error instead of errorMessage).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240-1244: Change to `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-email-error" when validation fails, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with that id because errorId is not passed, and the error message is passed via the wrong prop name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1266-1270: Change to `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when validation fails, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with that id because errorId is not passed, and the error message is passed via the wrong prop name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1293-1297: Change to `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea has aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" when validation fails, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with that id because errorId is not passed, and the error message is passed via the wrong prop name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320-1324: Change to `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute misplaced on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the dialog element. This prevents assistive technologies from correctly identifying the modal boundaries and may allow users to navigate outside the modal while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1478: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the overlay div. Line 1480-1485: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the div with `role="dialog"`: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm Button references non-existent status element
issuesFound.explanation: The Button has aria-describedby="newsletter-status", but the element with that id is only rendered when status === 'success' (line 1016), while the Button is rendered when status !== 'success'. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: Remove aria-describedby={statusId} from the Button props

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm Button loading state not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component expects an isLoading prop to set aria-busy, but NewsletterForm passes loading={isSubmitting}. This results in aria-busy not being set, so screen readers do not announce the loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1042: Change loading={isSubmitting} to isLoading={isSubmitting}

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Button loading state not communicated
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component expects an isLoading prop to set aria-busy, but ContactForm passes loading={isSubmitting}. This results in aria-busy not being set, so screen readers do not announce the loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1162: Change loading={isSubmitting} to isLoading={isSubmitting}

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. This incorrectly scopes the modal property to the backdrop, potentially allowing assistive technology to escape the modal context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1239: Remove aria-modal="true" from the div. Line 1242: Add aria-modal="true" to the inner div with role="dialog"
