issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button references non-existent element via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has aria-describedby={statusId} pointing to "newsletter-status", but the element with that id is only rendered when status === 'success'. When the form is visible (status !== 'success'), the button exists but the referenced element does not, creating a broken ARIA reference that confuses screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1768: Remove aria-describedby={statusId} from the Button component.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrectly bears aria-modal attribute instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div rather than the element with role="dialog". This causes assistive technologies to incorrectly identify the modal boundary and may fail to treat the dialog as modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1924: Remove aria-modal="true" from the modal-overlay div. Line 1926: Add aria-modal="true" to the div with role="dialog" (after role="dialog").

issuesFound.title: Skip to search link targets element absent on mobile viewports
issuesFound.explanation: The "Skip to search" anchor in SkipLinks targets #site-search, but the SearchInput component containing that id is conditionally rendered only when isMobile is false. On mobile devices, activating the skip link does nothing because the target is not in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362: Remove the third skip link (href="#site-search") or conditionally render it based on the isMobile state matching the SearchInput visibility.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls linking to the answer panel, but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the panel is currently expanded or collapsed. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the current state of the accordion item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error state but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of the input field to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 477: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div handles click events to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabindex, and keyboard support, making the close action unavailable to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1110: <div role="button" aria-label="Close navigation" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" />

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls the visibility of the mobile-nav-panel dialog using the isOpen state variable but does not communicate its expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: Navigation dropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls the visibility of the submenu (nav-menu-*) using the isOpen prop but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the submenu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation section toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The section toggle buttons in the mobile navigation control expandable submenus using the isExpanded state but do not indicate their expanded state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls the visibility of the answer panel using the isOpen prop but does not communicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only a character symbol (‹) and lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only a character symbol (›) and lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button for the mobile navigation panel contains only a symbol (✕) and lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Close navigation" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field as erroneous but does not programmatically convey the invalid state to assistive technology via aria-invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not set aria-invalid to communicate the error state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the textarea.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not set aria-invalid to communicate the error state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the select.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error element ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but no element with that ID exists in the DOM because FormGroup is not passed an errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to FormGroup, or remove the aria-describedby from the input.

issuesFound.title: Contact form name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references 'contact-name-error' via aria-describedby, but the error message element is never rendered with this ID because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" and errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} to FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the error element is never rendered with this ID.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references 'contact-subject-error' via aria-describedby, but the error element is never rendered with this ID.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" and errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} to FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: Contact form message field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references 'contact-message-error' via aria-describedby, but the error element is never rendered with this ID.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" and errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} to FormGroup.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons behave as toggle buttons indicating the active filter but do not communicate their pressed state to assistive technology via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button.

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons indicate the selected billing period but do not communicate which option is selected via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the monthly button and aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the annual button.

issuesFound.title: Statistics section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar is a section element without an accessible name, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section.

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan article element lacks an accessible name to identify it among other plans in the grid, despite having a heading with an ID available.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article.

issuesFound.title: Skip links navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation landmark lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation landmark lacks an accessible name, which is needed because there are multiple nav elements on the page (footer, social, etc.).
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Main" to the nav.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation panel body missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile dialog lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label="Mobile navigation" to the nav.

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the explicit "required" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the asterisk.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading), it does not communicate this busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button.

issuesFound.title: Loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative loading spinner inside the button is not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant announcements alongside the loading text provided by VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the spinner span.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation is visually indicated with a class but not programmatically identified via aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor.

issuesFound.title: Mobile active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation lacks aria-current="page" to indicate it represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor.

issuesFound.title: Star rating widget missing accessible semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating widget lacks role="img" on the container, and the individual star characters are not hidden from assistive technology, potentially causing redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Add role="img" to the outer span and aria-hidden="true" to the inner star spans.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay click target missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space, or remove the onClick and rely on the dedicated close button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop click target missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard handlers.
issuesFound.fix: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler, or remove onClick and use the dedicated close button only.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute misplaced
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element, which may cause assistive technology to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Remove aria-modal from the overlay div and add it to the dialog div (the element with role="dialog").
